motivation: Enable the forked CLI project to compile/run locally and connect to a custom Anthropic-compatible API proxy via default user config, then clarify how to assess project “progress” and what evidence should drive ongoing optimization (vs. continued reliance on upstream/decompiled sources).
approach: Inspected the repo to confirm the supported config mechanism (`~/.claude/settings.json` → `settings.env` → `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`), then validated the runtime by building and executing the CLI against a proxy endpoint. Separately, proposed an evaluation/governance framework (reachability, smoke, stub-free hot path, bundle behavior parity, regression, etc.) and concrete artifacts (COMPLETENESS_MATRIX/DIVERGENCE/smoke scripts) to guide future work without re-reading decompiled sources.
challenges: Misconfiguring `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` with a `/v1` suffix caused requests to become `/v1/v1/messages` and fail; an alternate proxy endpoint returned 502, so only the working endpoint could be used for verification.
tradeoffs: Chose behavior-based validation (smoke/E2E + bundle behavior parity + divergence tracking) over exhaustive re-diffing against decompiled/upstream code, accepting that non-critical stubs/feature-flagged modules can remain dormant until they affect hot paths.
outcome: Confirmed the correct configuration path and that the base URL must be the root (no `/v1`), then successfully executed an end-to-end CLI request through the proxy. Established a practical, WHY-driven rubric for future development: prioritize making P0/P1 hot paths reachable + smoke-tested + regression-protected, and track intentional behavior deviations from the original bundle explicitly (rather than chasing line-level source parity).
